items.title: SaaS Dashboard
items.summary: Dense but calm interface for repeated scanning, comparison, and action.
guidance: Use a persistent sidebar, compact toolbar, and table-first information architecture.
Surface status, owner, time, and next action without hiding key metadata behind hover.
Prefer restrained neutrals, one accent for actions, and clear empty/loading/error states.

items.title: E-commerce Storefront
items.summary: Conversion-focused product browsing with trustworthy product detail.
guidance: Lead with inspectable product imagery, price, availability, and primary action.
Use filters, sort, swatches, size/state controls, and cart feedback as first-class UI.
Avoid decorative layouts that delay product comparison.

items.title: Portfolio Site
items.summary: A first-viewport identity signal followed by concrete work evidence.
guidance: Make the person, studio, or product name the headline signal.
Show a real project thumbnail or artifact above the fold.
Use case studies with role, constraints, process, and outcome.

items.title: Fintech Mobile App
items.summary: High-trust financial flows with clear risk, confirmation, and recovery paths.
guidance: Prioritize balances, transaction status, identity, and confirmation copy.
Use conservative color semantics for gain, loss, warning, and disabled states.
Design fraud, lock, timeout, and verification states before polish.

items.title: Healthcare Portal
items.summary: Calm, accessible workflows for appointments, results, and care instructions.
guidance: Use plain language, visible next steps, and strong contrast.
Keep appointment time, provider, location, and preparation details together.
Avoid playful ambiguity around status, alerts, or medical instructions.

items.title: Education Product
items.summary: Learning interface that makes progress, feedback, and next activity obvious.
guidance: Pair content hierarchy with progress, completion, and revision states.
Use examples, practice blocks, and review checkpoints instead of long text walls.
Make assessment feedback specific and recoverable.

items.title: AI Developer Tool
items.summary: Technical product UI that values traceability, logs, configuration, and repeat use.
guidance: Use split panes for prompt/config, output, logs, and traces.
Expose model, token, latency, version, and error metadata when relevant.
Use monospace only where it adds parsing value.

items.title: Clean Enterprise
items.summary: Work-focused style with low visual noise and strong hierarchy.
guidance: Use white or near-white surfaces, soft borders, and 8px or smaller card radius.
Prioritize alignment, spacing, and predictable controls over decorative graphics.
Reserve accent color for primary actions and selected states.

items.title: Glassmorphism With Control
items.summary: Use glass only when the background remains legible and performance is acceptable.
guidance: Limit glass panels to overlays or hero moments; do not make every card translucent.
Keep text on solid or strongly blurred surfaces with contrast checked.
Use one depth system: shadow, blur, and border should agree.

items.title: Editorial Product
items.summary: Strong type, large images, and measured rhythm for narrative pages.
guidance: Use oversized type only for true page-level moments.
Pair large media with short copy and clear section rhythm.
Keep interaction controls conventional even when visual direction is expressive.

items.title: Playful Consumer
items.summary: Expressive UI with motion, illustration, and friendly copy.
guidance: Use rounded shapes and saturated accents deliberately, not everywhere.
Make primary workflows faster than the decoration.
Animate feedback and state changes; avoid animation that blocks action.

items.title: Dark Command Center
items.summary: Dark operational UI for dense status monitoring.
guidance: Use dark neutrals with clear elevation and high-contrast data colors.
Avoid low-contrast gray text for operational values.
Use color consistently for health, warning, error, paused, and unknown.

items.title: Luxury Minimal
items.summary: Sparse layout, strong material imagery, and slow confidence.
guidance: Use fewer objects, larger margins, and high-quality imagery.
Avoid generic gradients and overused stock backgrounds.
Use accent color sparingly; material, texture, and typography carry the brand.

items.title: Inter + Fraunces
items.summary: Clean body text with a distinctive serif display voice.
guidance: Use Fraunces for page headlines or selected section titles.
Use Inter for UI controls, body copy, tables, and labels.
Keep body at 16px or above on marketing pages and 13-15px in dense tools.

items.title: Space Grotesk + Inter
items.summary: Contemporary technical voice with readable interface text.
guidance: Use Space Grotesk for short titles, numbers, and nav brand text.
Use Inter for longer copy and form-heavy screens.
Avoid negative letter spacing; let the typeface carry the tone.

items.title: IBM Plex Sans + IBM Plex Mono
items.summary: Reliable product type system for developer tools and data-heavy UIs.
guidance: Use Plex Sans for UI and Plex Mono for code, IDs, and logs.
Keep monospace blocks line-height generous enough for scanning.
Use tabular numbers in metrics where available.

items.title: Playfair Display + Source Sans 3
items.summary: Elegant editorial pairing for image-led pages.
guidance: Use Playfair only for display headings and short quotes.
Use Source Sans 3 for readable body copy and controls.
Balance high-contrast type with generous line height.

items.title: Manrope + Lora
items.summary: Warm professional pairing with readable UI and softer editorial moments.
guidance: Use Manrope for navigation, cards, forms, and dense panels.
Use Lora for articles, intros, and reflective sections.
Keep form labels plain; do not use serif type inside compact controls.

items.title: SaaS Clarity
items.summary: Trustworthy light SaaS palette with clear action emphasis.
guidance: Use blue for primary action, teal for positive status, amber for attention.

items.title: Fintech Trust
items.summary: Conservative financial palette with high contrast.
guidance: Reserve green for confirmed gain or completion; use red only for loss or destructive states.

items.title: Healthcare Calm
items.summary: Soft but legible healthcare palette.
guidance: Do not use low-contrast mint text; keep clinical data on neutral surfaces.

items.title: Commerce Warm
items.summary: Warm commerce palette that keeps product imagery primary.
guidance: Use warm backgrounds sparingly so product colors remain accurate.

items.title: Dark Data
items.summary: Dark UI palette with clear data state colors.
guidance: Keep secondary text at #CBD5E1 or brighter on dark surfaces.

items.title: SaaS Landing Structure
items.summary: Practical SaaS page structure that leads from offer to proof to conversion.
guidance: Hero: literal product/category headline, one-sentence promise, product screenshot, primary CTA.
Follow with problem, workflow, integrations, proof, pricing, FAQ, and final CTA.
Keep at least a hint of the next section visible below the first viewport.

items.title: Portfolio Home Structure
items.summary: Identity-first portfolio structure with visible work evidence.
guidance: Hero: name, role, specialty, and one strong project image or live artifact.
Follow with selected work, capabilities, testimonials or outcomes, writing, contact.
Avoid vague value props without real work samples.

items.title: App Launch Page
items.summary: Launch page that explains the job, shows the app, and gives a fast path to install.
guidance: Use device screenshots or product video, not abstract gradients.
Show top use cases, social proof, privacy/trust notes, and store badges.
Keep install CTA visible after the hero.

items.title: KPI Dashboard
items.summary: Metric layout for operational scanning.
guidance: Use four to six top KPIs with trend delta, time range, and definition.
Pair summary cards with a table or trend chart below.
Use color only for semantic status, not arbitrary decoration.

items.title: Time Series Trend
items.summary: Best for change over time and comparison against a baseline.
guidance: Use line or area chart with clear x-axis intervals and visible hover state.
Annotate major events instead of explaining them in long prose.
Do not use pie charts for time series.

items.title: Funnel Conversion
items.summary: Shows drop-off across ordered stages.
guidance: Use stage labels, absolute counts, conversion rate, and drop-off delta.
Make the largest loss visually obvious and explainable.
Do not compare unrelated funnels without normalization.

items.title: Cohort Retention
items.summary: Heatmap layout for retention or repeat behavior by cohort.
guidance: Use month/week columns and cohort rows with a single color ramp.
Freeze labels and include sample size.
Avoid rainbow palettes; they hide trend continuity.

items.title: Responsive Density
items.summary: Move from dense desktop scanning to focused mobile tasks.
guidance: Desktop can show tables and side panels; mobile should prioritize one task per screen.
Use stable grid tracks, aspect ratios, and min/max sizes for fixed-format elements.
Never rely on viewport-scaled font sizes to make copy fit.

items.title: Accessible Interaction States
items.summary: Every interactive control needs visible state and keyboard reachability.
guidance: Design default, hover, active, focus-visible, disabled, loading, success, and error.
Do not communicate state with color alone.
Make focus rings visible against both light and dark surfaces.

items.title: Empty Loading Error
items.summary: Operational screens need recovery states, not blank holes.
guidance: Empty state: explain why it is empty and offer the next action.
Loading state: preserve layout dimensions to prevent shifting.
Error state: show what failed, what data is safe, and how to retry.

items.title: Motion With Purpose
items.summary: Motion should clarify causality and status.
guidance: Use short transitions for expansion, filtering, navigation, and async feedback.
Avoid long decorative motion in repeated operational workflows.
Respect reduced-motion settings when implementing code.

items.title: HTML + Tailwind
items.summary: Fast static UI implementation with utility classes.
guidance: Use semantic HTML first, then Tailwind utilities for layout and states.
Define repeated component classes only when duplication becomes risky.
Use responsive prefixes to change layout, not just shrink text.

items.title: React
items.summary: Component implementation with explicit state surfaces.
guidance: Model loading, empty, error, optimistic, and success states in component props.
Keep presentational components close to their data shape unless reused.
Use existing design-system primitives before adding new abstractions.

items.title: Next.js
items.summary: Product pages and app screens with routing and server/client boundaries.
guidance: Use server components for static data and client components for interaction.
Keep first viewport meaningful with real product media or useful app UI.
Optimize images and avoid layout shift with width, height, or aspect ratio.

items.title: Vue
items.summary: Declarative interface implementation with composition API patterns.
guidance: Use computed values for display state and small composables for shared behavior.
Keep template conditionals readable; extract components when sections diverge.
Bind aria and disabled states together with visual state.

items.title: Svelte
items.summary: Compact component implementation with built-in transitions.
guidance: Use transitions for local state changes only when they clarify cause and effect.
Keep stores for shared state; keep simple local UI state in components.
Avoid animation that conflicts with reduced-motion needs.

items.title: SwiftUI
items.summary: Native Apple interface with dynamic type and platform controls.
guidance: Use platform-native navigation, lists, forms, sheets, and toolbars.
Respect Dynamic Type, safe areas, and system colors unless brand-critical.
Verify light/dark mode and accessibility sizes.

items.title: React Native
items.summary: Cross-platform mobile UI with explicit platform differences.
guidance: Use touch targets of at least 44px and avoid hover-only affordances.
Check safe areas, keyboard avoidance, and low-end device performance.
Use native navigation patterns where they differ by platform.

items.title: Flutter
items.summary: Cross-platform UI with strong layout primitives.
guidance: Use constraints, flex, and intrinsic sizing deliberately to avoid overflow.
Treat text scale factor as a required QA path.
Theme components centrally, then tune per screen.

items.title: shadcn/ui
items.summary: Composable app UI with accessible primitives.
guidance: Use Radix-backed controls for dialogs, menus, popovers, tabs, and selects.
Customize tokens and density before inventing new component behavior.
Keep forms and tables utilitarian, predictable, and keyboard-friendly.

items.title: Design Brief Prompt
items.summary: Minimal brief shape for UI generation or critique.
guidance: Include product type, target user, primary workflow, visual tone, constraints, and stack.
Name required states: loading, empty, error, success, disabled, and responsive breakpoints.
Ask for concrete layout, component behavior, copy hierarchy, and verification steps.
